project.name: WorldOS Frontend
project.description: Next.js 16 + React 19 TypeScript frontend for WorldOS V6 dashboards, simulation visualization, 3D world views, real-time logs, and social/narrative panels.

nodes.name: Next.js App Router
nodes.summary: SSR-friendly App Router structure with client shell wrappers for dashboard interactivity.

nodes.name: Dashboard Shell
nodes.summary: DashboardShell client wrapper preserves SSR in layout while enabling tabs, state, and real-time UI.

nodes.name: React Query API Layer
nodes.summary: TanStack React Query hooks fetch backend simulation state and cache server data.

nodes.name: Real-time Centrifugo
nodes.summary: Centrifugo WebSocket client streams simulation/log updates into dashboard components.

nodes.name: Simulation Visualization
nodes.summary: 3D/graph/chart visualization stack using React Three Fiber, ReactFlow, and Recharts.

layers.name: Configuration & Build
layers.description: Next.js, TypeScript, ESLint, PostCSS, Vitest, and package configuration.

layers.name: App Router
layers.description: Next.js App Router pages, layouts, and route-level files.

layers.name: Dashboard Components
layers.description: WorldOS dashboard UI, tabs, shells, panels, visualization components, and controls.

layers.name: Shared Components
layers.description: Reusable UI components outside dashboard-specific views.

layers.name: Data Hooks
layers.description: React hooks and TanStack Query wrappers for backend API state.

layers.name: Client Libraries
layers.description: Axios API client, Centrifugo WebSocket factory, log parsing, and frontend utilities.

layers.name: Types & Static Data
layers.description: Shared TypeScript domain types and static data assets.

tour.title: Frontend Project Setup
tour.description: Start with package and framework configuration to understand dependencies and build tooling.

tour.title: App Router Entry
tour.description: Follow Next.js layout/page bootstrapping into the dashboard experience.

tour.title: Dashboard Shell
tour.description: DashboardShell coordinates tabs, client state, layout composition, and real-time dashboard interaction.

tour.title: Dashboard Tabs
tour.description: Explore decomposed tab components that represent major WorldOS views: overview, world map, social graph, narrative, actors, and logs.

tour.title: Data Fetching Layer
tour.description: TanStack Query hooks call the Axios API client to fetch simulation data from Laravel backend endpoints.

tour.title: Real-Time Updates
tour.description: Centrifugo WebSocket integration streams live simulation and log events into the frontend.

tour.title: Visualization Stack
tour.description: 3D world, graph, and chart components use React Three Fiber, ReactFlow, and Recharts.
